storyInfo: One hundred and forty years ago, the previous True Lung took the throne. He summons Wang and demands that he eliminate Sui in twenty years, in order to free up the massive energy spent keeping Sui suppressed. Jie meets Chun, her new Candleholder.

attributes.content: This is as far as we can go, Your Imperial Majesty.
You are the only one in all of Yan who may enter the room beyond this door.
It is but a door. There is no need to be so fearful.
It conceals Yan's greatest secret, and its greatest asset, Your Imperial Majesty...
...Is that so?
The seventeen-year-old True Lung walks down the last few steps.
He looks back at the three Grand Ministers behind him, who are still bowing deeply. He frowns and pushes the door open.
He has heard about what lies behind the door. The legends and stories of this ancient nation that have been passed down through the centuries—
'The Earth's blessings have granted this stone to its people; the Heaven's blessings shall bring an end to Sui.'
Some say that the prosperity of Yan comes from it. Others say the True Lung's authority is merely borrowed from it.
But no one has seen it with their own eyes in nearly a century.
The ascending True Lung is filled with excitement, his fingertips trembling.
Everything in the land of Yan shall obey him, for the prosperity of Yan—
...So will one small crystal.
He pushes the door open.
As ambitious as he is, he could not help but stop before the massive space and blinding light.
It is not the first Originium itself, but man-made additions upon it, the limiters and guidance that generations after generations of Tianshis have placed upon it over the centuries.

attributes.text: At the new capital of Baizao, a tomb was built to hold the vessel of Sui, and the consciousness of Sui was suppressed by the power of calamity. Yet the Emperor was not at ease, and so he sought counsel from the Tianshi Bureaus in hopes of keeping the peace for eternity hence.

Then the Emperor discovered the means to harness the power of calamity, but it would take the lives of a hundred Tianshis and ten thousand soldiers to form the blade and slay the beast. Even if complete success were achieved, Baizao would not be spared.
And the Emperor said, 'It is shameful to bring harm to the people for one's own purposes. I shall give up myself, and my descendants shall do the same, and not demand the sacrifice of the people.'
The True Lung passed the next year, and by it the enchantment was complete. The crown prince grieved and named it 'Bu Fan'.

attributes.content: ...Bu Fan, 'No Rebellion'. Sounds like 'No Return' too.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: My ancestor sacrificed himself and made the reigning True Lung the subject of future human sacrifice. Henceforth, the True Lungs that came after him gave their lives to harness the power of this Originium.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: I would not have known it was so massive if I did not see it for myself...
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Yet it's no more than a tiny crystal compared to all of Yan and Yan's future.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Yan could have done much more with this object.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: How much energy have we spent on suppressing the sleeping beast? With such energy, how many armies could we have raised, how many machines could we have built?
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: How could I tolerate such... inaction?
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: The True Lung lingers before the facility for a while. He has gotten used to the scorching light, and even starts to feel... boring.
A smile comes to his face as he remembers the reverence on his three Grand Ministers' faces. How will he deride their timidity when he leaves the room without being rude?
He recalls the 'rule' that kept the ministers out, for him to face this structure alone.
The rule that inextricably links it to his life.
He could not help but laugh.
The True Lung's authority over all under heaven, tied to a tiny crystal like this?
I am still inexperienced. I will be relying on the three of you for many things.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Yan has been peaceful and prosperous for centuries. Why is it that there are still rebellions?
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Is there still land where the blessings of Yan do not touch? Or seditious subjects who will not submit to imperial rule?
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Silence.
I will decide myself, then, as you do not answer.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: The blessings of Yan shall touch every corner, so that not a single person shall worry for food or shelter. The teachings for Yan shall reach every heart, so that not a single rebellious thought will enter the mind of a single man.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: We shall vanquish the northern traitors and build the nomadic city of Baizao. Both are the foremost priority, and neither shall be delayed.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: You shall summon the Six Ministries, discuss the specifics, and deliver a proposal by the end of the month.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Dismissed.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: The trio on the other side of the curtain bow in assent and leave.
A little later...
Ah, you have arrived.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: ...It is an honor, Your Imperial Majesty.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: I've long heard of the twelve 'extraordinary talents' of Yan, credited with no small number of achievements for the empire. I've been curious for some time.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: I therefore perused the Sui Regulator's archives, and found something rather interesting.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Be it reports of triumph, or suspicious activities...
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: No name has been mentioned more often than yours.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: But I see your visage is not as terrifying as the rumors claim.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Why has Your Imperial Majesty summoned me?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: They call you the most cunning strategist in all Yan.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: How do you propose to resolve the two great obstacles we face?
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: The northern rebellion is hardly a major threat. The rebels may be influential in their local area, but they are powerless to expand. Nevertheless, it would be inadvisable to allow them to consolidate their position.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Fifty-thousand soldiers and six months' worth of rations will be sufficient to defeat them.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Your estimate is much more optimistic than the Ministry of War... but I have more reason to trust your judgment on this.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: What about the other matter?
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: As for the modification of Baizao into a nomadic city... I understand the Ministry of Works has validated the engineering principles. The only issues are funding, supplies, and...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: ...the Tomb of Sui beneath Baizao.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Yes. You understand the problem.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: It is for this that I have summoned you.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: How do you intend to eliminate Sui?
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: If you are familiar with the scrolls of the Sui Regulator... you understand that 'we' were the same and one as Sui, and our fates intermingled.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Do you mean to ask how to kill 'us'?
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: It is only 'Sui' that Yan is concerned about, and no other being.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: You understand, of course, the price that Yan paid for the hunt that occurred centuries ago.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: The price that must be paid, if Yan is to force the issue, is seven-tenths of its armies, one-tenth of its population, and fifty years of unceasing unrest.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: If that is the case, Yan will be in no position to ensure the safety of the twelve of you.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: The proxy looks up and meets the gaze of the man behind the curtain.
I am determined to cleanse Yan of this thousand-year bane. I am not afraid to pay the price—any price.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: The Sui will be destroyed. The tremendous energy that was used to suppress that beast shall be reutilized to build a hundred cities and ten thousand battleships, and usher in another thousand years of prosperity for Yan.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: I had hoped that there would be a place for the twelve of you in Yan's future prosperity, on account of your past deeds. It is regrettable.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: If your decision to eliminate Sui comes from practical judgment, rather than reckless pursuit of glory...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: ...I may have a way.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: Oh?
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: The twelve of us were one and same as Sui. We were destined to fade, whether It awakes or is destroyed.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: But it is possible that I could... replace It.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: How sure are you?
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: I will need time...
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: The young man behind the curtain points at the courtyard outside.
That ginkgo sapling was planted in the spring of this year.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: The Ministry of Works swore an oath in this hall several days ago that the rebuilding of Baizao into a nomadic city will be complete before the tree bears fruit.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Twenty years. That is the time you have.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: That is too little time.
attributes.name: Wang

attributes.content: I am not here to bargain.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Immortality has made you arrogant. Perhaps you have forgotten that mortals do not have many 'twenty years'.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: Yan cannot wait, and neither can I.
attributes.name: True Lung

attributes.content: You're the new Candleholder? So much time has passed, in the blink of an eye.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Has the previous Candleholder...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: She retired last month, and asked me to tell you not to worry.
attributes.name: Quiet Candleholder

attributes.content: I will take over your communications with the Sui Regulator from now on.
attributes.name: Quiet Candleholder

attributes.content: Well, you'll have your hands full.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Most Candleholders have to worry about tracking down my free-roaming siblings. As for mine...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: They have to sit in this office all day, handling books and scrolls. They are more like secretaries than Candleholders.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I enjoy reading and writing. This is right up my alley.
attributes.name: Quiet Candleholder

attributes.content: Perfect.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: What's your name?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I'm Chun.
attributes.name: Quiet Candleholder

attributes.content: 'Chun'?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: 'For the ancient tree that shares your name, eight thousand years is a spring and eight thousand years is an autumn...' Was the one who named you remarkably ambitious, or remarkably cautious?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Wait, you are...?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: ...An Elf?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Yes.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Judging from your appearance... you're an Elf of Yaan?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: The bestial Yaan and its Elves are well-versed in the ways of Yin-Yang and the Five Elements. The element associated with spring is wood, and the character for 'Chun' combines both 'wood' and 'spring', representing vitality and longevity... I see.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: My mother told me about it when I was young. I didn't expect anyone else to know.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Stuff like this is what history is about.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: It's your first day. I won't put you to too much hard work. Just familiarize yourself with the space; you can look at any books and documents in this study.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: The Candleholder carefully scans the room. She could not conceal her eagerness as she sees scrolls and books that fill the wall shelves.
Did you write all of these?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: No, I simply collect the materials. The historians of the Imperial Historical Cabinet are the ones who actually write the books.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: After all...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Is writing history a difficult task?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: It's a heavy responsibility.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: History books form people's perception of the past, but a few words are hardly enough to give them a complete view.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Choices must be made as to which parts to record for posterity, what sorts of lessons those who come after should learn...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: It's easy to say, 'write with an impartial pen,' but it is a most profound and complex discipline.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: What if there is a mistake in the history books?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Then it should be corrected.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: History is inevitably warped by the position of the narrator, no matter how impartial the writer attempts to be.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I see a few more problems because I've lived longer than most and remember more than most.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Most... it's true that your kind, the proxies, are different from other long-lived. Before I joined, I heard that each of you had your own 'powers'.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Do you use your powers to correct the errors in the history books?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: I heard you have a magical writing knife... do you use it to correct those errors?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: It can, but this is not how it should be used.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: ...Huh?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Mistakes take root in people's hearts as they are passed down from one generation to another. The proper way to fight them is to form a new consensus through debate and clarification, not to simply erase it.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Erasing a wrong word from one's own writing is not a significant issue, for it only concerns oneself.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: To do it to a published book would be to meddle with the memories of thousands.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: As for history... setting aside the question of how much I can alter, it will not be a good thing, for myself and for Yan.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Frankly, it's already a chore to ensure that history is not altered. I hardly have the energy to alter it myself.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Hm...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Seems like there's something you would like to change with the knife yourself.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Huh?! I—
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Before my mother passed away, she said I may be the last of the Yaan Elves.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: She... she wanted me to find a home.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: I wandered for many years. I tried many ways to blend in, but I always failed. Yan does not want—
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Yan does not want to accept you, because the Yaan Elves were depicted in the Great Hunt as... 'fickle and disloyal.'
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: But I know that's not true...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: The bestial Yaan stood against Yan simply to survive. That cannot be denied.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: The Elves, though, had only wanted to mediate the conflict, yet they became the enemy of both sides... ultimately, most were killed in battle, and their deeds lost to time.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: So you wanted to alter this history?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I don't deny that I thought about it. But I'm your Candleholder now, and my work comes before all else.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: I can't lend you the writing knife, nor can I allow you to use it to alter history.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I understand. I shouldn't have—
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: But if we can find proof that the Elves were in fact not disloyal, I will do all that I can to help you correct the erroneous records, in the proper manner.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Huh?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Things haven't been too busy lately. We could visit the area where you used to live. What do you think?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I... I've only just come. Besides, the things I said aren't necessarily true. I—
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: I can tell that your passion for words and history is genuine. Besides... who will believe you, if the historian does not?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Yes... yes!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: This is the key to my office. You're my Candleholder now. Take good care of it.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: How do you get in, if you give me the key?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Why should the master of the house need a key?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: The court building has only just been completed! Why...?
attributes.name: Judicial Officer

attributes.content: Don't worry, it will not be torn down. Our Tumu Tianshis will transplant the Judicial Review Office to the nomadic plate, undamaged.
attributes.name: Ministry of Works Official

attributes.content: But wouldn't it have been so much cheaper to build it directly on the nomadic plate?
attributes.name: Judicial Officer

attributes.content: I hear the imperial court is putting everything behind this 'nomadic city'. I'm sure our cooperation will be very important.
attributes.name: Greedy Shopkeeper

attributes.content: Get to the point. What do you want?
attributes.name: Tired Official

attributes.content: My rosewood chair, ebony table, Kou-wu plates and Danyan lamps... none of them were inexpensive.
attributes.name: Greedy Shopkeeper

attributes.content: Here are the receipts from when I ordered my things. If there is any damage...
attributes.name: Greedy Shopkeeper

attributes.content: Fine, fine. You'll be reimbursed accordingly.
attributes.name: Tired Official

attributes.content: Yes, the prisoners have been transferred.
attributes.name: Confused Guard

attributes.content: The previous instructions were from the Judicial Review Office, while the new ones are from the Ministry of Justice?
attributes.name: Confused Guard

attributes.content: I understand, but am I supposed to conjure the prisoners out of thin air?
attributes.name: Confused Guard

attributes.content: You don't care? The burdenbeast carriages are arriving in minutes?
attributes.name: Confused Guard

attributes.content: Minister. Please excuse the poor hospitality. I did not know you were visiting.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I have never seen you in the Sky Mirror Pavilion. I take it you have important business?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: There isn't much time, Miss Jie. I'll get straight to the point... there is an error in this year's archives that I would like to ask you to correct.
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: A mistake in the documents that I audited? Pray tell.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: The documents submitted by the Ministry of Works state there were around a hundred deaths associated with the Chidao project at Danyan this year. Why did the number become three thousand?
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: The figure of three thousand deaths was not cited from a single document, but calculated based on documents from the Ministry of Works, the Ministry of Revenue, and local government departments.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: The records are carefully sourced. I have kept a copy, should you wish to review them.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: While it is not my place to question the work of the Ministry of Works, I have no idea why the three thousand deaths in Danyan's local records became just a hundred when it reached Baizao.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: You must submit sufficient evidence, however, should you wish to question the documents audited by the Sky Mirror Pavilion.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Ahem... well, Danyan is not so well-staffed as Baizao, and there are only limited resources available for record-keeping.
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: Besides, the cold climate there causes disease and frostbite in winter. Perhaps not all of the three thousand died in the course of constructing the Chidao...
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: I see. You wish for me to alter the documents to make the Ministry of Works look better before the cabinet.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: This is not about the cabinet. This is about Yan.
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: You know about all the projects being carried out across Yan. They push the nation's resources to the limit, but there is no doubt that these projects will benefit the nation and its people.
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: I'm not the only one who feels that such... depressing figures will weaken the people's morale.
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: I don't deny the benefits of these measures. Precisely because of that, the three thousand people who died to build the Chidao gave their lives for their country. It would be an insult to their memory to reduce it to a hundred.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Unless you can prove that there has been an error, any text that I have reviewed must not be altered. Anyone else who has a complaint can bring it to me directly.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: The nation must be united in the monumental task of building the nomadic city. Everyone at the court is doing their best, and each faces their own challenges...
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: Yet the writings of the Imperial Historical Cabinet and the Sky Mirror Pavilion have been most... aggressive and deprecating. It is as though you wish for work on the nomadic city to stop immediately!
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: Is it the writer who has something to hide, or the reader?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: How dare you...!
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: The imperial court has compensated the next of kin in full! Not a single coin has been withheld!
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: I have noted this in the documents that I approved. The care that the imperial court has shown towards those injured and killed, as well as their families, illustrates Yan's love towards its people.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Then why do you cling to a single number like this?!
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: I understand your concern for Yan, minister. But I worry that people will only see the glory of the accomplishments, and not the people whose sacrifices made it possible.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: All I can do is to ensure that the history I see is recorded exactly as it is.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: And you think you alone can decide the history of Yan?
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: History has never been decided by one person. It is the combined soundless voices of millions.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: You are a learned man in a position to improve the lives of thousands upon thousands, minister. I simply leave behind some words for the people whose voices will never be heard.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Now, ask your conscience, and answer again for those people...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: How many died building Danyan's Chidao this year? Was it a hundred... or three thousand?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: (Mutters irritably)
attributes.name: Minister of Works

attributes.content: The Minister of Works just stormed off in a huff... is it because of Danyan?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Is this why you have come, sister?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I can't imagine how they managed to convince you to lobby on their behalf... am I actually dreaming, while the one in the Tomb of Sui has awakened?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Are you so sarcastic to everyone these days?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I came to tell you that the Sui Regulator and the Ministry of Justice have approved your request to clear the Yaan Elves' name.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I'm sorry. I was in a bad mood.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Apologies accepted.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: That's the first good news I've heard in days. It will make Chun less gloomy, I hope.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: It's not good to be fighting with the Sui Regulator, but I doubt they would be happy to see you get too friendly with your Candleholder.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I understand. But she's really taken to her work, helping me compile the histories... she clearly likes it.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I have a feeling... she didn't become a Candleholder because she wanted to, but because she was alone and didn't have a choice but to take the hand that was offered.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Well, a wrong has been righted. That's always a good thing.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Of course, I think part of their intention is to get you to compromise on changing the documents.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I simply came to discuss matters, because this matter has gotten to the three Grand Ministers and the True Lung. I'm not lobbying on anybody's behalf.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Just treat it as the guy in the Tomb of Sui throwing a tantrum.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: You're the only one in the family who jokes about this.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Ling has honeyed words, and you speak with heaven's authority. I'll settle for the frivolous tongue, then.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: It's true that the officials of the court are facing a dilemma in this matter.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: The figure of three thousand will cast a shadow upon the nomadic city project, and the implications it may have for Yan are unknown.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Did you tolerate the falsification of records while you were still administering justice?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Falsifying records is a crime, of course. However, if the perpetrator did not do it out of selfish interest, I will consider extenuating circumstances when determining the punishment.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I'm surprised to hear that.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: We each have our own specializations. I don't work in history, and you don't work in law.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Perhaps that is how matters of law work... but not in history.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I wanted to study history and language because I wanted to learn what the human mind is truly like.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: The answer to my questions may lie in the intention behind words, and the truth behind history...
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Not for Yan, nor for the land, not even for our family... only for my own questions—
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Jie! Jie!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Chun? What are you doing here? I wasn't ready to tell you—
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Tell me what?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Never mind... what's the hurry?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: I went to the study to get the drafts as you asked, but I discovered signs of an intrusion!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: The writing knife on the desk is gone!
attributes.name: Chun
